verses.text: Nebuchadnezzar publishes to all peoples the 'signs and wonders' the most high God wrought toward him, whose kingdom is everlasting. A king's testimony. Even the proudest can be brought to praise God.
Nebuchadnezzar publishes to all peoples the 'signs and wonders' the most high God wrought toward him, whose kingdom is everlasting. A king's testimony. Even the proudest can be brought to praise God.
Nebuchadnezzar publishes to all peoples the 'signs and wonders' the most high God wrought toward him, whose kingdom is everlasting. A king's testimony. Even the proudest can be brought to praise God.
At ease in his palace, he dreams a terrifying dream the magicians cannot interpret. Peace disturbed. God can shake a man in the midst of his security.
At ease in his palace, he dreams a terrifying dream the magicians cannot interpret. Peace disturbed. God can shake a man in the midst of his security.
At ease in his palace, he dreams a terrifying dream the magicians cannot interpret. Peace disturbed. God can shake a man in the midst of his security.
At ease in his palace, he dreams a terrifying dream the magicians cannot interpret. Peace disturbed. God can shake a man in the midst of his security.
At last Daniel comes, in whom is 'the spirit of the holy gods,' and the king tells him the dream. The true interpreter. God's servant is sought when all others fail.
At last Daniel comes, in whom is 'the spirit of the holy gods,' and the king tells him the dream. The true interpreter. God's servant is sought when all others fail.
He saw a great tree reaching to heaven, sheltering beasts and birds, its fruit feeding all. Towering greatness. The dream pictures the king's vast dominion.
He saw a great tree reaching to heaven, sheltering beasts and birds, its fruit feeding all. Towering greatness. The dream pictures the king's vast dominion.
He saw a great tree reaching to heaven, sheltering beasts and birds, its fruit feeding all. Towering greatness. The dream pictures the king's vast dominion.
A watcher cries, Hew down the tree but leave the stump, and let his heart be a beast's for 'seven times.' Sentence pronounced. Pride is to be humbled to the dust.
A watcher cries, Hew down the tree but leave the stump, and let his heart be a beast's for 'seven times.' Sentence pronounced. Pride is to be humbled to the dust.
A watcher cries, Hew down the tree but leave the stump, and let his heart be a beast's for 'seven times.' Sentence pronounced. Pride is to be humbled to the dust.
A watcher cries, Hew down the tree but leave the stump, and let his heart be a beast's for 'seven times.' Sentence pronounced. Pride is to be humbled to the dust.
This is decreed 'to the intent that the living may know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men.' The lesson. God humbles the proud that all may see who truly reigns.
This is decreed 'to the intent that the living may know that the most High ruleth in the kingdom of men.' The lesson. God humbles the proud that all may see who truly reigns.
Daniel, troubled, tells him: the tree is thou, O king, grown great, thy dominion reaching the earth's end. Faithful truth. He does not flatter, but speaks the hard word with care.
Daniel, troubled, tells him: the tree is thou, O king, grown great, thy dominion reaching the earth's end. Faithful truth. He does not flatter, but speaks the hard word with care.
Daniel, troubled, tells him: the tree is thou, O king, grown great, thy dominion reaching the earth's end. Faithful truth. He does not flatter, but speaks the hard word with care.
Daniel, troubled, tells him: the tree is thou, O king, grown great, thy dominion reaching the earth's end. Faithful truth. He does not flatter, but speaks the hard word with care.
Thou shalt be driven from men to dwell with beasts and eat grass, till thou know the most High rules. Coming abasement. The mighty king will be brought lower than the lowest.
Thou shalt be driven from men to dwell with beasts and eat grass, till thou know the most High rules. Coming abasement. The mighty king will be brought lower than the lowest.
Thou shalt be driven from men to dwell with beasts and eat grass, till thou know the most High rules. Coming abasement. The mighty king will be brought lower than the lowest.
The stump means the kingdom shall be restored; 'break off thy sins by righteousness,' that thy peace may be lengthened. Mercy offered. Repentance might yet have averted the blow.
The stump means the kingdom shall be restored; 'break off thy sins by righteousness,' that thy peace may be lengthened. Mercy offered. Repentance might yet have averted the blow.
A year later, walking in his palace, he boasts, 'Is not this great Babylon, that I have built... for the honour of my majesty?' Pride speaks. Self-glory ripens just before the fall.
A year later, walking in his palace, he boasts, 'Is not this great Babylon, that I have built... for the honour of my majesty?' Pride speaks. Self-glory ripens just before the fall.
A year later, walking in his palace, he boasts, 'Is not this great Babylon, that I have built... for the honour of my majesty?' Pride speaks. Self-glory ripens just before the fall.
While the word is in his mouth, the sentence falls; he is driven out to eat grass, his hair like eagles' feathers. Judgment strikes. Boasting is cut short at the height of its folly.
While the word is in his mouth, the sentence falls; he is driven out to eat grass, his hair like eagles' feathers. Judgment strikes. Boasting is cut short at the height of its folly.
While the word is in his mouth, the sentence falls; he is driven out to eat grass, his hair like eagles' feathers. Judgment strikes. Boasting is cut short at the height of its folly.
At the end he lifts his eyes to heaven, his reason returns, and he blesses the most High, whose dominion is everlasting. Humbled and restored. Looking up to God is the beginning of recovery.
At the end he lifts his eyes to heaven, his reason returns, and he blesses the most High, whose dominion is everlasting. Humbled and restored. Looking up to God is the beginning of recovery.
His honour returns, and now he praises the King of heaven, 'those that walk in pride he is able to abase.' A changed king. The hardest heart, broken, learns to glorify God.
